skill.name: chembl-database
skill.description: Query the ChEMBL database for bioactive molecules, targets, bioactivities, and approved drugs. Use this skill when you need to filter by physicochemical properties such as molecular weight and LogP, search by chemical structure using SMILES, or retrieve drug mechanism information.
skill.summary: Search and analyze bioactive molecules, drug targets, and bioactivity data from the public ChEMBL database.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 53 static analysis findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The static scanner incorrectly identified Markdown code formatting backticks as Ruby shell backtick execution (42 findings), misidentified argparse calls and JSON audit notes as weak cryptographic algorithms (5 findings), and misidentified benign JSON notes as system reconnaissance (2 findings). The skill legitimately uses the official chembl_webresource_client library to query the public ChEMBL API at ebi.ac.uk. No malicious patterns, obfuscated code, or data exfiltration vectors were found. The network access and Python script execution are documented and necessary for the skill's intended function of querying a public drug database.

low_findings.title: Markdown code backticks misidentified as shell execution
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged markdown code formatting backticks (used for inline code and code fences in SKILL.md and api_reference.md) as Ruby shell backtick execution. These are documentation examples, not executable commands. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: argparse calls misidentified as weak cryptography
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged argparse.ArgumentParser() calls in query_chembl.py as weak cryptographic algorithms. These are standard Python CLI argument definitions. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: Source URL misidentified as hardcoded network endpoint
low_findings.description: The source attribution URL in SKILL.md (line 7) links to the GitHub repository. This is documentation, not a network call or data exfiltration endpoint. FALSE POSITIVE.

content.user_title: Query ChEMBL for Drug Discovery Research
content.value_statement: Drug researchers waste hours manually browsing the ChEMBL website to find bioactive molecules and target data. This skill lets you search the ChEMBL database by molecule name, chemical properties, or target proteins and get structured results instantly.
actual_capabilities: Search for molecules by preferred name or synonym with case-insensitive matching
Filter compounds by physicochemical properties such as molecular weight and LogP
Retrieve target protein information and link targets to their known ligands
Fetch bioactivity measurements including IC50, Ki, and EC50 values
Look up drug mechanism-of-action data for approved and annotated drugs
limitations: Requires internet access to connect to the public ChEMBL API at ebi.ac.uk
Results are limited by the ChEMBL API schema and available data fields
Structure-based SMILES search depends on API endpoint capabilities and may not be supported for all queries
Bioactivity data completeness varies by compound; not all records contain standardized fields

use_cases.title: Find Drug Candidates by Property Filters
use_cases.description: A medicinal chemist searches for compounds with molecular weight under 500 and LogP below 5 to identify drug-like candidates for a new project.

use_cases.title: Retrieve Target-Ligand Bioactivity Data
use_cases.description: A pharmacology researcher looks up all IC50 values for a specific compound against cyclooxygenase targets to support structure-activity relationship analysis.

use_cases.title: Explore Approved Drug Mechanisms
use_cases.description: A competitive intelligence analyst retrieves mechanism-of-action records for approved drugs in a therapeutic area to map the treatment landscape.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Molecule Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the ChEMBL database for molecules matching the name 'aspirin' and show the top 5 results including their ChEMBL IDs and preferred names.

prompt_templates.title: Property-Filtered Compound Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find molecules in ChEMBL with molecular weight less than 400 and a LogP value below 3. Show the top 10 results with their properties.

prompt_templates.title: Target and Bioactivity Lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for targets containing 'COX-1' in ChEMBL. For each target found, retrieve associated ligands and their IC50 bioactivity data.

prompt_templates.title: Mechanism of Action Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Find approved drugs in ChEMBL that target the dopamine receptor. Retrieve their mechanism-of-action records including action type and mechanism description.

output_examples.output:
Found 3 molecules matching 'aspirin':
- CHEMBL215 (Aspirin / Acetylsalicylic acid) - MW: 180.16, LogP: 1.2
- CHEMBL12345 (Aspirin analog) - MW: 210.23, LogP: 1.8
- CHEMBL67890 (Aspirin derivative) - MW: 194.19, LogP: 1.5

output_examples.input: Find targets containing 'cyclooxygenase' and their bioactivity data
output_examples.output:
Target: Cyclooxygenase-1 (CHEMBL221)
- Known ligands: 45 compounds with activity data
- Sample IC50: Compound CHEMBL215 has IC50 of 3.5 uM

Target: Cyclooxygenase-2 (CHEMBL230)
- Known ligands: 78 compounds with activity data
- Sample IC50: Compound CHEMBL215 has IC50 of 15.0 uM

best_practices: Start with a broad name search before applying property filters to understand the available data landscape
Use case-insensitive substring matching (icontains) for molecule and target name searches to catch variant spellings
Limit result sets with slicing (e.g., [:5] or [:10]) to manage API response size and processing time
anti_patterns: Do not assume all compounds have complete bioactivity data; always check for missing standard_type or standard_value fields
Do not use the skill for real-time clinical decision support; ChEMBL data is research-oriented and may not reflect current medical knowledge
Do not query without filtering when searching broad terms like 'kinase'; results will exceed practical limits and time out

faq.question: What is the ChEMBL database?
faq.answer: ChEMBL is a public database of bioactive molecules with drug-like properties maintained by the European Bioinformatics Institute (EMBL-EBI). It contains data on millions of compounds and their interactions with biological targets.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. The ChEMBL API is publicly accessible without authentication. The chembl_webresource_client library handles all connection details automatically.

faq.question: What types of bioactivity data can I retrieve?
faq.answer: You can retrieve standard bioactivity measurements including IC50, Ki, EC50, Kd, and potency values along with their units and assay context.

faq.question: How recent is the data in ChEMBL?
faq.answer: The public ChEMBL API serves the latest released version of the database. Updates are published periodically by EMBL-EBI.

faq.question: Can I search by chemical structure?
faq.answer: Structure-based search using SMILES strings may be supported depending on the API endpoint. This skill primarily supports name-based and property-based searches.

faq.question: What are the rate limits for the ChEMBL API?
faq.answer: The public ChEMBL API has rate limits to ensure fair usage. The official client library includes built-in caching that reduces duplicate requests.
